reviews.review_description: Despite the very short demo, it's evident that this is going somewhere. Yes, it's not perfect, yes, it's quite short, even for a demo, and yes, there's a lot to work on, but the groundwork that does exist perfectly demonstrates the potential that this game ALREADY has. The movement feels great, there are well-thought out mechanics, and the systems that exist make it stand out from so many other VR arena titles. Although the game isn't quite 5 stars yet, it shows great promise, and I know if the devs continue to show and pour passion into this project, this'll be something special. Keep it up, devs!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the love! We're continuing to work on improvements and appreciate the feedback!

reviews.review_description:
This isn't the normal type of game I gravitate too but It looked so interesting , I had so much fun I was late for my appointment. This demo started pulling me into the world then the demo ended ;)
The graphics are great, I'm definitely going to give it a shot when it comes out. It is truly a beautiful game.

I played for about 20 minutes and generally enjoyed the game. The movement is extremely smooth and I greatly appreciate how each of the spells have a unique effect on movement. My biggest complaint is there is really no reason to mix up what spells you are using besides the ones you like the best/most powerful. Lightning feels a bit underpowered from what I played, but I truthfully didn't fully understand how the merge attack worked so that could effect it. I would also love to see more spells in the game or ways to modify your spells further like a skill tree or mutations.

reviews.review_title: I'm not sold on it yet
reviews.review_description:
First impressions:

I played for around fifteen minutes so far and it's generic and a bit boring.

Everything has a very familiar look. Cliche "world floating in a void" with shiny accents and lines everywhere.  Standard sci fi video game stuff that I've seen in many other games.

As someone else said the shooting doesn't feel right. I'm assuming that'll be tweaked in the full release.

Hopefully the full version won't pin text to the viewport. Even with the Q3's pancake lenses it's awkward to read text that moves with your head. I imagine with the Q3S's fresnel lenses it may difficult reading the text at the edges of the lenses where they're the blurriest.

I'm not motivated to play through the whole demo but I will.  I hope it gets better

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We updated the HUD text to be more readable for VR. Would love to hear more specific feedback on shooting if you wouldn't mind dropping into our Discord server.

reviews.review_description: ABSOLUTE CINEMA. SOOOOOO GOOD just 4 stars cos i really want Xbox support so other than that amASING. Also im on Q3S and i read most of the reviews and i don't think the text is blurry only on the part that doesn't have text

reviews.review_title: So far just ok beautiful game tho
reviews.review_description: Has a unique look to it and unfortunately that’s about it I found the shooting to awkward didn’t like how’s that worked I only played for about 10 min tho I could see with some work this game could be a 7 out of 10 but as of now it’s just an ok game hope that when the game fully comes out it has better graphics because if it’s like 15 /20 bucks I might pick it up

developer_response.body: We've made a bunch of improvements to graphics ands performance since the initial demo release. We would love to hear more specific feedback on shooting if you would like to drop into our Discord server. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: Okay, now this is a tight little demo you got going, shame if some of the text cant be moved to where it can be read on Quest 3S, because the text towards the "bottom and edges" of my "screen" are blurry due to the lenses, I had to vigrously nod my head to read it all. Gameplay wise oi feels weird, but Ill quickly get used to it. Please tell me youll offer different double jump modes, the first one does the jump, second dashes, Id like to fine tune that.  So far this feels like Devil Daggers in VR, and Im loving it!

developer_response.body: Appreciate the feedback! We updated the HUD text to be more readable in VR. Most of the movement tech was designed to be built into the spells themselves. Check out the latest update!

reviews.review_description:
Bro the movement is amazing and I have only played for like 3 minutes and it is already the greatest demo i have ever played expecily in vr amazing (
also choose fire)

Yes the demo is short but it grabs you and makes you want more , 

This will be a day one for me

reviews.review_title: Amazing game look forward to it

reviews.review_description: THIS IS AMAZING but one thing that would be nice is health pickups in areas I have lost alot of runs to lack of health pickups and quick question will the game be free when it launches?
This is a very exciting game. Something that's defrent  .it's very fast playing. I'm looking forward to playing the release. Something new in vr
Really excited for the full release of this one!

reviews.review_title: Not a VR game, a game with VR.
reviews.review_description: This intrigued me when I saw in the store as something that maybe finally uses VR mechanics to create new magic systems, but unfortunately after trying this - this ain't it. The game is clearly built for flatscreen first and should be played on flatscreen. As far as I have seen in this demo - VR does nothing for this game but immensely reduce the graphics in standalone. I first tried it on Steam and promptly dropped it there because I could not adjust my VR controls. On standalone at least I could switch off the snap turn and had to fiddle quite a bit to adjust the turn speed (most games that is not a problem at all), but there does not seem to be option for seated mode while the game has no real justification why I should be standing while playing it. And the gameplay - it's just a shooter masquerading as a magic game.  It includes the hand control gimmicks just for the sake of "Look, mom, I got hands!" but there is really no need for that. If you are into it, go for it, but VR is just afterthought for this. Personally - I want to see less of this stuff in VR and more actual VR games, more developers developing with the strengths of the VR, not for it as a gimmick.

developer_response.body:
Totally get the criticism. For what it's worth, the game is actually built for VR first, despite the limited VR specific interactions. From our side, we wanted to make a game that played equally on flatscreen and VR, and focused on balancing the two modes so they can compete with each other on the leaderboards. What VR adds for a game like ours is increased spatial awareness and more dynamic control of the two hands for aiming compared with flatscreen. The differences may be subtle, but they give a pretty distinct advantage and unique feel.

Also, we will work on improving settings for both platforms. Appreciate the feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Shows promise in some areas, but completely  lacks a sense of purpose.

A little guidance or a tutorial would be nice. What the hell am I doing? Nobody knows, including me.
